page: The Vultr vx1-m-48c-384gb is a virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 48 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and no dedicated GPU is included.

Benchmark results indicate strong processing and memory capabilities. The AMD EPYC-Turin processor achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core tests and gzip compression, alongside strong performance in multi-core operations and Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with large-block read and write speeds reaching top-tier thresholds. In database and caching scenarios, the server demonstrates strong capabilities, processing 87.64 million Redis SET operations per second and scoring in the top 25% for Passmark database operations.

A primary tradeoff of this instance is its shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained, co-tenant workloads. Additionally, the local storage is restricted to a minimal 1 GB network volume, requiring external storage solutions for data-intensive applications. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial memory capacity, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores.

This configuration is suited for large-scale memory-cached databases, Redis deployments, static web serving, and moderate-scale language model inference. It is also applicable for memory-heavy data processing tasks and general-purpose workloads requiring high memory density without the cost of dedicated compute resources.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-48c-384gb is a memory-dense virtual server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 384.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio, though it is constrained by a shared CPU allocation and a minimal 1 GB network storage volume. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core and database processing capabilities. It processes 87.64 million Redis SET operations per second and shows strong performance in static web serving. Given its high memory capacity and shared CPU model, this server is well-suited for memory-cached databases, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and general-purpose workloads requiring high memory density.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384gb is a VX1 Cloud Compute server with 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384gb is a VX1 Cloud Compute server featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: A high-memory virtual server featuring forty-eight shared AMD EPYC cores and three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
384.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 core-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth and single-core CPU performance
1 GB network-attached local storage with no GPU hardware
Suited for Redis caching, database operations, and static web serving
